pattern: A monotonic stack walks the array charging one operation whenever a strictly higher value opens above the current run, while zeros reset the stack and equal heights ride for free.
hints: Each operation zeros the current minimum across a chosen subarray, so think about how many distinct rising levels you must knock down.
Scan left to right keeping a stack of active heights; a zero clears everything and a height equal to the stack top continues the same level without a new operation.

When the new value exceeds the stack top (or the stack is empty after popping larger heights) it begins a fresh level that needs one operation; pop heights greater than the current and count only genuinely new levels.

edge_inputs.description: all equal positives are one operation

stress.description: medium array with small values and scattered zeros
stress.note: small range and zeros exercise resets and equal-height runs; kept moderate for the O(n^2) oracle
